summary: This PR graduates the `recover-mental-model` capability from a stub placeholder into a real, production-ready protocol by replacing the `recover-mental-model-stub` directory with a fully-wired `recover-mental-model` protocol that orchestrates three parallel evidence-gathering legs: `legion` (codebase mapping via `/legion:map`), `codeset` (codebase vibing via `python -m codeset`), and `socratic` (a two-phase agent sub-pipeline with a human-gate answering step). Each leg gets its own evidence schema, form-only deterministic check, and artifact-naming convention; the three trees are merged by a new `push-mental-model.py` publish hook that assembles them onto an orphan `_mental_model` branch. Alongside the protocol, the previously named `rmm-summary-agent` workflow is repurposed and renamed to `mm-codeset-agent`, gaining a codeset-vibing run step (checking out the PR head, running `python -m codeset`, and emitting evidence) rather than a prose-summary task, while the gh-aw compiler is bumped from v0.77.5 to v0.80.9 with updated action pins, a new daily AI-credits guardrail, and minor workflow scaffolding improvements throughout all compiled lock files.
